zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Salmesbury於大約1256年出生在英國藍卡爾薩苗萊斯波利時，她的父親William de Samlesbury38歲，母親Avina Notton38歲。她於1272年與Sir Robert de Holand II在英國英格蘭藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: de

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Salmesbury was born about 1256, in Samlesbury, Lancashire, England, her father, William de Samlesbury, was 38 and her mother, Avina Notton, was 38. She married Sir Robert de Holand II in 1272, in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Salmesbury est née environ 1256, à Samlesbury, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, William de Samlesbury, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Avina Notton, avait 38 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Robert de Holand II en 1272, à Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Salmesbury(이)가 대략 1256년 영국 랭카실르 샘레스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Samlesbury(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Avina Notton(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 1272년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르에서 Sir Robert de Holand II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Salmesbury родилась около 1256 г. в местности сэмлесбури, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Samlesbury было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Avina Notton, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert de Holand II в 1272 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери.
